title: Aquatic assault stormtrooper

'''Aquatic assault stormtroopers''', also known as '''seatroopers''' or '''aquatic troopers''', were a specialized branch of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. They were used by the Galactic Empire to maintain a presence on the many aquatic [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] under its rule. It is unknown if they had any ties to the [[Imperial Maritime Division|aquatic Imperial navy]].

==Equipment==
[[File:Seatroopers-BftGS.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Seatroopers battling [[Sedrian]]s on [[Sedri/Legends|Sedri]].]]
{{Main|Seatrooper armor}}
Many of the standard technologies employed by the Imperial military were modified for aquatic use, including specialized aquatic [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]], custom [[TIE fighter boat]]s, and powerful [[Aquatic Terrain Armored Transport|AT-AT swimmers]]. The armor of the seatrooper was based on the lightweight scout trooper design. It was worn over a two-piece environmental body glove that provides protection from uncomfortable temperature extremes, or toxic-water environments. Also, the scout trooper helmet was modified to accommodate oxygen tubes and filter out any harmful chemicals in harmful environments for the added adaptability of the aquatic sea trooper corps.
Designed specifically for engagements on [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] and [[Tibrin/Legends|Tibrin]], the seatrooper's armor was pressure-sealed to a maximum of ten atmospheres and included a rebreather that allowed to stay underwater for more than a hour.<ref name="RECG" /> The armor was more flexible than standard stormtrooper designs, preserving and even increasing a trooper's underwater dexterity. Supplementing a soldier's swimming skills are a back-mounted propulsion unit, and a pair of propulsion boots with snap-down flippers. Since blaster ranges are often adversely affected by underwater use, the seatroopers carried a blaster rifle/speargun hybrid as a standard weapon. In [[137 ABY]], the seatroopers of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] used [[Plasma rifle/Legends|plasma rifles]].

The standard seatrooper [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]] includes high-tension wire, [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hooks]], spare blaster [[Power cell/Legends|power packs]], ion flares, concentrated [[Ration pack/Legends|rations]], spare [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]], [[Rebreather/Legends|rebreather]] filters, and a compressed air inflatable bubble tent.

Seatroopers also used the [[SG-4 blaster rifle]] which was capable of firing miniature harpoons underwater.<ref name="RECG" />

==History==
[[File:Seatroopers_Iskalon_Effect.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Seatroopers on Iskalon during the Galactic Civil War]]
Aquatic assault stormtroopers were the successors of the [[Clone SCUBA trooper/Legends|clone SCUBA troopers]] that participated in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] as part of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].

A variation was seen on the planet [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] some time after [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. This variation consisted of a regular stormtrooper helmet and upper body armor over heavy white coveralls. The back-mounted propulsion unit also incorporated an air supply, but the propulsion boots did not have flippers.<ref name="SW74">[[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]]</ref> Thousands of aquatic stormtroopers were used by the Empire to conquer [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]].<ref name="Manaan: Depths of History">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040205manaan1|series=Manaan: Depths of History|text=Part 1: Backwater}}</ref>

Seatroopers, along with representatives from every specialized [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]], were assigned to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] personal [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]].<ref name="GG3">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> At some point after the Battle of Endor, in large part due to the seatroopers' prowess in aquatic combat, the Rebel Alliance mobilized the [[Mon Calamari Knights/Legends|Mon Calamari Knights]] to aid in the liberation of various aquatic worlds, since they were uniquely qualified to deal with the seatroopers.<ref name="Imperial Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>

[[File:AlphaSquadron.jpg|thumb|right|[[Alpha Squadron (seatrooper)|Alpha Squadron]] of seatroopers during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]].]]
During the [[Second Imperial Civil War]] Aquatic assault stormtroopers were also employed by the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] on the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] for the [[Genocide on Dac|extermination]] of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] people ordered by [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Darth Krayt]].<ref name="Legacy 2006 32">[[Legacy (2006) 32|''Legacy'' (2006) 32]]</ref> Following the overthrow of the [[One Sith]], the seatroopers came under the control of the [[Galactic Federation Triumvirate]], a new galactic government consisting of the [[Fel Empire]], the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]], and the [[New Jedi Order]]. Seatroopers serving under the [[Imperial Knight]] [[Yalta Val]] took part in the [[Liberation of Mon Calamari Shipyards|liberation]] of Mon Calamari and [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] slaves who had been coerced into building ships for a pirate syndicate led by the rogue Sith [[Darth Luft]]. Seatroopers played an important role in apprehending the pirates, who were subsequently handed over to the authorities.<ref name="Legacy 2013 10">[[Legacy (2013) 10|''Legacy'' (2013) 10]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Seatroopers first appeared in the [[Marvel Comics]] story [[Star Wars (1977) 74|''Star Wars'' (1977) 74]], which was first released on [[May 17]], [[1983]]. Seatroopers subsequently appeared in several [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] Expanded Universe literature including the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', ''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'', and the [[Dark Horse Comics]] series ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 2]]''.
